atlas_description: Family / Individual evidence hub — consumer of ngsPedigree outputs (pairwise_relationship_classification.tsv, family_hub_roster.tsv, per-chromosome QC, chromosome inheritance map) plus an inversion karyotype TSV from the Inversion Atlas. 226-sample pure C. gariepinus hatchery cohort.

_paired_with._doc: Producer/consumer pair. ngsPedigree is the analysis side (Stage 1 graph annotator, Stage 2 per-chromosome QC, Stage 3 chromosome inheritance map). The relatedness atlas is the consumer / browser UI. The legacy Relatedness_atlas.{html,js} files live in BOTH repos (ngsPedigree/pages/ and relatedness-atlas/) and are kept byte-identical; the relatedness-atlas/atlases/ tree is the migration target. Same producer/consumer pattern as inversion-popgen-toolkit + inversion-atlas, catfish-diversity-analysis + diversity-atlas, etc.

_round_1_note: First migration round. Single stage 'hub' with five pages (one per sub-tab of the legacy Relatedness_atlas.html mockup): network, karyotypes, inversions, mendelian, compatibility. Each page extracts the matching §6-§9b section from the legacy Relatedness_atlas.js. Shared chrome (Population Browser tree §4, Population Summary §5, Inspector / Stats §10, Loaded files §11) lives in shared/ and is rendered on every page mount. Registry content (requires_layers / requires_slots / preloads) is OUT-OF-SCOPE per the kickoff rule (see 0_READ_ME_FIRST.md) — registries only carry _label + _doc + atlas_id this round.
_migrated_out_2026_05_23: Phase 1b 2026-05-23: six meiosis-flavored pages moved to meiosis-atlas (meiosis -> observables, eligibility, resolution, coincidence, inversion_signature, focal_meiosis_scan). Fragment + module files now live at atlases/meiosis/pages/hub/. The migrated pages still share THIS atlas's state singleton via cross-atlas imports (they read `../../../relatedness/shared/state.js` rather than declaring their own state). Reasoning: relatedness owns pedigree/breeding/Mendelian-distortion content; meiosis owns CO/NCO/coincidence/interference observables. focal_meiosis_scan's own tooltip already labelled it 'Transient home: seed page of the future Meiosis Atlas' — that planned move is now complete.

pages.label: network
pages.tooltip: PO / FS / ambiguous / Mendelian-conflict edges in the current hub. Click any node to centre the evidence hub on that individual.

pages.label: karyotypes
pages.tooltip: Per-sample genotype calls at every inversion candidate. Scan ancestry composition alongside karyotype patterns.

pages.label: inversions
pages.tooltip: All inversion candidates from the Inversion Atlas — filter by status, chromosome, length, frequency.

pages.label: mendelian
pages.tooltip: Mendelian segregation tester. Pick a dyad or triad, choose inversion subset, run binomial / multinomial. The headline feature.

pages.label: compatibility
pages.tooltip: Breeding-partner finder. Pick a focal individual + target karyotype + scope; lists compatible partners with sex-aware / exclude-kin filters.

pages.label: BDMI screen
pages.tooltip: Bateson-Dobzhansky-Muller incompatibility screen. Runs six population-genetic tests per inversion candidate (Mendelian distortion, missing class, het deficit, ancestry interaction, long-range forbidden combinations, phenotype association) and assigns a confidence ladder weak/moderate/strong/very-strong.

pages.label: regime inheritance
pages.tooltip: Chromosome regime inheritance — tests whether a focal inversion's distortion is explained by linkage to another inversion on the same chromosome (haplotype-regime coupling), then classifies the residual signal as meiotic-drive-like vs underdominance-like via cross-type comparison. Prevents over-calling BDMI / drive / underdominance from coupled inversions.

pages.label: priority
pages.tooltip: Inversion priority table — Pass-1 (WGS discovery) → Pass-2 (marker validation at scale) bridge. One row per inversion with composite priority_score and SHIP/HOLD/DROP bucket. Use to pick the 5-20 inversions to take into the next 10k-50k-fish marker round.

pages.label: marker designer
pages.tooltip: Interchromosomal Marker Test Designer — converts a candidate interchromosomal meiosis effect into a practical marker panel: focal inversion classifier markers in parents + transmission/recombination markers on tested chromosomes for offspring panels (CO/DCO/coincidence).

pages.label: family deep-dive
pages.tooltip: Family-hub deep-dive — per-hub roster, sex + ancestry composition, within-hub edges, triad QC, karyotype informativeness. Pure pedigree-side. Use to decide whether a hub is large enough / clean enough to anchor a downstream analysis.

pages.label: catalogue
pages.tooltip: Atlas-core Catalogue handshake / self-test — reads the four JSONL files exposed to the master workflow catalogue and verifies the smoke-test constraints client-side. Diagnostic, not analytical.

pages.label: sample portrait
pages.tooltip: Per-individual portrait — single-sample dossier consolidating identity, ancestry vector, pairwise relationships, karyotype profile, triad role, inversion burden, cross-page jump-links. Read-only aggregation.

pages.label: candidate dossier
pages.tooltip: Per-inversion dossier — single-candidate aggregation: identity, karyotype distribution + HWE, carrier roster, family-hub spread, Mendelian distortion, same-chrom companions, downstream-analysis readiness.

pages.label: cohort
pages.tooltip: Cohort summary — single-screen overview of the 226-sample hatchery cohort: scale, sex, ancestry, families, candidates, frequency spectrum, call rate, edges, per-analysis runnability matrix.

pages.label: cross planner
pages.tooltip: Batch cross planner — multi-pair extension of Compatibility. Generates every candidate parent pair under chosen filters, ranks by composite cross_utility_score, flags inheritance-side warnings (heterokaryote → CO suppression, redundancy, hub confounding, close-kin). Seed of the breeding-AI advisor.

pages.label: compare
pages.tooltip: Side-by-side comparison of 2–4 inversion candidates: identity, karyotype distribution + HWE, hub spread, Mendelian Test A p, regime context, marker readiness. Use when narrowing down which inversions ship to Pass-2 marker validation.

pages.label: export
pages.tooltip: Atlas-wide export bundle. One click → one file containing all 7 cohort-wide sections (cohort overview, per-individual, per-candidate, per-family, inversion priority, marker designs for top-N, focal × meiosis scans for top-N). TSV or JSON. Per-page TSV exports remain available for analyses that need user choices.

server._doc: Relatedness Atlas does NOT ship its own server binary — it consumes the atlas-core unified server (atlas-core/server/atlas_server.py) via three contracts: (1) static reads of the assembled workspace for ngsPedigree data files; (2) sandboxed reads via GET /file/{path}; (3) POST /compute/<name> dispatch for Mendelian / Compatibility computes (see atlases/relatedness/server/RELATEDNESS_ENDPOINTS.md for the per-endpoint contract — the source of truth for the sister chat working on atlas_server.py). The atlas-side falls back to in-browser compute when an endpoint is missing, so the atlas is fully usable today without the server-side handlers.

_stylesheets_doc: Atlas-wide stylesheet, extracted verbatim from the legacy Relatedness_atlas.html <style> block. Future work: split into per-page stylesheets (css/pages/<page>.css) and reference them from each page entry's `stylesheet` field — the router loads page-stylesheet on mount.

scope_pickers.label: chromosome
scope_pickers._doc: Chr filter rendered as a select inside the legacy #subTabBar. In the round-1 single-file mock all 28 chromosomes (Chr01..Chr28) are populated dynamically from DEMO.chromosomes. Promoted into AtlasState so the cross-tab filter persists across page switches.

stages.label: evidence hub
stages._doc: Family / Individual Evidence Hub — the five sub-tabs of the legacy mockup. Future rounds may add a 'review' stage for manual call decisions and a 'catalogue' stage for the per-family roster export.

cross_atlas._doc: In/out adapter declaration. Relatedness reads ngsPedigree outputs (its analysis-side pair) PLUS inversion karyotypes + population kinship. Publishes a pairwise relationship classification for inversion + meiosis to consume.

imports.purpose: Per-candidate karyotypes for the relatedness karyotypes sub-tab.
Haplotype regimes for the regimes sub-tab (regime inheritance).
Pairwise theta for the inversion-aware kinship recompute.
Genome-wide PCA — relatedness uses for ancestry baseline.
Cohort-level F_ROH x H plane assignments — context for the inbreeding sub-tab.
ROH segments — inbreeding context for the eligibility sub-tab.

exports.purpose: Per-pair relationship class (PO, FS, HS, FC, unrelated). Inversion uses for sample-level QC; meiosis uses for trio detection; population uses for the families page.
Per-family roster (founders + offspring + siblings). Meiosis especially needs trio identification.
Per-sample karyotype calls at every inversion candidate. Used by relatedness.karyotypes + cross-atlas by inversion + meiosis.
Per-chromosome QC flags from ngsPedigree Stage 2.
Per-sample ancestry Q-vector at canonical K. Meiosis uses for stratifying CO/NCO calls.
